personas.name: Test Engineer
personas.description: QA or test automation engineer who writes and maintains end-to-end tests. Frustrated by flaky tests caused by timing issues, screenshot-based assertions that miss data correctness, and CSS selector fragility. Wants deterministic, fast, and semantically meaningful test assertions.
goals: Replace screenshot-based verification with structured state queries
Eliminate flaky tests caused by fixed timeouts and race conditions
Verify data linkage chains (filter change → API call → table update) in one assertion
Run the same test across multiple device profiles without rewriting
Get clear, actionable error messages when pre-checks fail (element not found, disabled, busy)
pain_points_addressed: Cannot verify data correctness from screenshots — only visual appearance (~80% accuracy)
Fixed setTimeout waits cause both slowness and flakiness
CSS selectors break on refactors without semantic meaning
No way to verify that a filter change actually triggered the right API call
Tests pass/fail inconsistently due to timing-dependent assertions
personas.interaction_pattern: Uses integration SDK (ProbePage for Playwright, ProbeTester for Flutter, ProbeXCTest for iOS, ProbeTestRule for Android, ProbeDevice for Detox). Writes test code against the typed probe API. Relies on waitForState/waitForPageReady for synchronization and actAndWait for composite operations. Uses custom matchers (toHaveProbeState, toHaveProbeData, toBeEffectivelyVisible) for readable assertions.
key_api_methods: query() — state assertion instead of screenshot
verifyLinkage() — explicit data flow chain validation
personas.success_metric: 60-page acceptance testing reduced from 2+ hours to 15-30 minutes; flaky tests eliminated by event-driven waits

personas.name: Frontend Developer
personas.description: Developer building the UI who adds probe annotations to key controls. Needs the annotation overhead to be minimal (5-10 annotations per page, <30 minutes) and zero-impact in production builds.
goals: Annotate key UI controls with minimal effort (<30 min per page for typical complexity)
Use platform-native idioms (HTML attributes, SwiftUI modifiers, Compose modifiers)
Ensure annotations have zero production impact (collector only injected at test time)
Express data linkages declaratively (data-probe-linkage JSON) for automatic verification
Get CLI support for auto-generating annotations on existing codebases
Validate annotation quality via CLI (no duplicate IDs, no orphan linkage targets)
pain_points_addressed: Test engineers constantly ask about element selectors and timing — probe gives them semantic access
Refactoring UI breaks tests written against implementation details
No standard way to express 'this dropdown controls that table' — linkage model solves this
data-testid attributes are arbitrary and don't carry semantic meaning
personas.interaction_pattern: Adds data-probe-* attributes (web), ProbeWidget wrappers (Flutter), .probeId() modifiers (SwiftUI/Compose), or attached properties (WPF/MAUI) to key UI controls. Uses CLI 'instrument' command to auto-annotate existing projects. Uses 'validate' command to check for duplicate IDs, orphan linkages, circular linkages. Uses 'report' command to check coverage.

personas.name: SDK Contributor
personas.description: Engineer implementing a new platform SDK (e.g. adding Electron support, implementing a stub platform). Must understand the full UITestProbe interface, the ProbeElement model, and the 6-primitive contract. Works against spec/probe-types.ts as the authoritative contract.
goals: Implement the complete UITestProbe interface (6 primitives + hierarchy + platform context)
Map platform UI tree (DOM / Widget Tree / View Hierarchy / SemanticsNode) to ProbeElement model
Achieve conformance with spec/probe-types.ts type definitions
Match annotation style to platform idioms while preserving semantic equivalence
Integrate with the platform test framework (WidgetTester, XCTest, ComposeTestRule, etc.)
pain_points_addressed: No conformance test suite yet (spec/conformance-tests/ referenced but not present)
ProbeType enum divergence (7 vs 9 types) between platforms is undocumented at SDK level
personas.interaction_pattern: Reads spec/probe-types.ts as the contract. Reads sdk/web/ as the reference implementation. Implements the same interface in the target language. Writes annotations module (parse/attach metadata), collector module (registry + state observer + source tracker), and actions module (dispatcher with pre-checks). Wires together in a main class.

architecture_contract.annotations_module: Reads platform-native metadata and produces ProbeElement fields
architecture_contract.collector_module: Maintains live registry; observes state changes; intercepts network
architecture_contract.actions_module: Dispatches real platform events with 5-point pre-checks and linkage verification
architecture_contract.main_class: Composes all modules; implements UITestProbe interface
